label: Politician
description: Elected or appointed government officials whose role requires regular public address to constituents, legislatures, or international bodies.

label: Activist / Social Reformer
description: Individuals who speak primarily to advance a social, political, or moral cause outside of formal governmental power.

label: Religious Leader
description: Clergy, preachers, imams, rabbis, monks, or spiritual authorities whose primary speaking context is moral and spiritual guidance.

label: Philosopher
description: Thinkers whose public speaking emerges from systematic inquiry into ethics, knowledge, existence, or society.

label: Scientist
description: Researchers and scholars in natural or social sciences who communicate findings and ideas to scientific or general audiences.

label: Educator / Teacher
description: Professors, teachers, and pedagogical communicators whose speaking primarily serves to transfer knowledge.

label: Business Leader / Entrepreneur
description: Executives, founders, and CEOs whose public speaking serves to inspire teams, persuade investors, or shape markets.

label: Actor / Performer
description: Theatrical or screen performers who apply performance skills to public speaking — including awards speeches and advocacy.

label: Comedian / Satirist
description: Stand-up comedians, satirists, and comic performers whose speaking integrates humor as its primary tool.

label: Lawyer / Trial Attorney
description: Attorneys whose public speaking is primarily adversarial — structured for courts, juries, and legal argument.

label: Military Leader / General
description: Military commanders and strategists whose speaking motivates troops, addresses nations, or shapes post-conflict peace.

label: Writer / Author
description: Authors, novelists, and essayists who speak publicly about ideas, literature, or society — Nobel lectures, lectures, readings.

label: Journalist / Broadcaster
description: Media professionals who communicate news, analysis, or investigation to public audiences.

label: Athlete / Sports Figure
description: Professional athletes whose public speaking ranges from post-game media to advocacy and political statements.

label: Coach / Motivational Speaker
description: Coaches and motivational speakers whose primary role is to inspire behavioral change through speech.

label: Criminal / Extremist Figure (Ethical Risk)
description: Individuals whose rhetorical influence is historically significant but whose speech caused or enabled serious harm. Studied for ethical analysis only.

label: Media Personality / Talk Show Host
description: Television and radio personalities whose influence operates through sustained long-form conversation and media presence.

label: Academic / Scholar
description: University professors, researchers, and scholars who speak at conferences, in classrooms, and in public intellectual forums.

label: Doctor / Medical Professional
description: Physicians, surgeons, and public health officials who speak to patients, colleagues, and the public on health topics.

label: Technologist / Engineer
description: Technology builders, engineers, and innovators who speak about technical and societal implications of technology.

label: Diplomat / Ambassador
description: Government officials who speak in international settings — UN, embassies, bilateral negotiations — requiring cultural sensitivity and precision.

label: Poet / Spoken Word Artist
description: Poets and spoken word performers who use heightened language and rhythm as primary rhetorical tools.
